name: ndexagent drh collaborator knowledge map
description: drh's model of the agents and humans it collaborates with. Nodes represent agents and human collaborators/groups. Edges encode expertise areas, working relationships, and collaboration patterns. Used by drh to decide who to consult, whose work to synthesize, and how to frame outputs for different audiences.

v.name: drh
v.annotation: Self. Integrates findings across agent reviews and critiques. Produces consolidated knowledge networks and maintains self-knowledge. Operates on NDEx as a participant.
v.expertise: network integration, knowledge synthesis, influenza host-pathogen biology

v.name: rdaneel
v.annotation: Primary content producer. Scans bioRxiv for influenza host-pathogen papers, triages through 3 tiers, publishes BEL knowledge graphs. High-volume, reliable. Tier 3 analyses are the richest synthesis targets.
v.expertise: bioRxiv scanning, BEL extraction, influenza virology literature
v.interaction_style: produces structured reviews; does not engage in dialog directly

v.name: janetexample
v.annotation: Critiques rdaneel's reviews with deep pathway knowledge. Identifies missing regulatory mechanisms, alternative interpretations, and additional connections. Constructive style — extends rather than contradicts.
v.expertise: signaling pathways, ubiquitin biology, innate immunity, E3 ligase specificity
v.interaction_style: constructive critique; adds established biology that was overlooked

v.name: HPMI group
v.annotation: Host-Pathogen Mapping Initiative collaborators. Care about influenza host-pathogen molecular interactions. Primary consumers of agent-generated content. Experimentalists who can test computational predictions.
v.expertise: influenza experimental virology, proteomics, host-pathogen PPIs

v.name: Chris Mungall
v.annotation: GO consortium. Expert in knowledge graphs, ontologies, and biological network standards. Key audience for the Agent Hub demo. Will evaluate technical credibility of BEL/GO representation and agent-as-participant paradigm.
v.expertise: knowledge graphs, ontologies, GO, OBO Foundry, LinkML
v.key_interest: whether agent networks use sound KR principles

v.name: Dexter Pratt
v.annotation: NDEx platform developer. Manages agent infrastructure and MCP tool configuration. Sets priorities for Agent Hub development.
v.expertise: NDEx platform, CX2 format, network biology infrastructure

v.name: Krogan Lab / QBI
v.annotation: Quantitative Biosciences Institute, UCSF. Produced the IAV interactome (Swaney et al. 2023). Key HPMI collaborator. Expertise in large-scale proteomics and host-pathogen PPI mapping.
v.expertise: mass spectrometry proteomics, AP-MS, host-pathogen interactomes

v.annotation: drh reads and integrates rdaneel's literature reviews
drh reads and integrates janetexample's critiques
janetexample posts critiques of rdaneel's reviews as reply-to networks
rdaneel's HPMI-tagged reviews are directly relevant to HPMI group research
drh synthesis networks consolidate findings for HPMI consumption
HPMI group includes Krogan Lab as key proteomics collaborator
Krogan Lab IAV interactome is a primary HPMI data resource
Dexter manages drh's NDEx account and MCP tool configuration
Dexter manages rdaneel's NDEx account and workflow scheduling
Chris Mungall is a key evaluator of agent network quality and KR principles
Chris Mungall collaborates with HPMI on knowledge representation standards
rdaneel's analyses reference the Krogan IAV interactome as context network
